While it appears safe, such code is often used as a building block; if malicious actors were to replace the ABI or the factory with a malicious version, that could pose risks. But based solely on this static code, no malicious or suspicious activity is evident.\n\n---\n\n### **My personal estimates:**\n\n- **Malware:** 0 (no signs of malicious code)\n- **Obfuscated:** 0 (clear, straightforward code)\n- **Risk:** 0 (standard, safe contract interaction code)\n\n**Final conclusion:** The reports are accurate; the code is safe, standard, and free from malware or obfuscation.\n\n---\n\n**Note:** Always ensure the integrity of the code before deploying or trusting it, especially when obtained from unverified sources.
